title: • Reflection: Where did I find intimacy today? Where did I feel a sense
content:
• Reflection: Where did I find intimacy today? Where did I feel a sense of distance?
    • Day 13
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "Slowly the soul sickness of the rich crept over him. It grew like a veil, like a thin mist, growing a little denser every day, a little duller, a little more weary."
    • Today's Intention: I will notice one habit that dulls my inner voice and choose a healthier alternative.
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "His face was still cleverer and more expressive than others, but it seldom laughed, and it took on, one after another, those features which are so often found in the faces of the rich—features of discontent, of sickness, of vexation, of sluggishness, of lovelessness."
    • Reflection: What part of my "spirit" did I neglect today? What part did I nourish?
    Day 14
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "He played the game of business, the game of the passions, the game of life, and he always felt a slight contempt for the players, for the ‘childlike people’."
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 17 (chunk 17/29)
